eventName: Building Production RAG Pipelines — Community Edition
description: A community-first workshop on RAG pipelines with plenty of networking, ice-breakers, and group exercises. Designed to be welcoming for developers at all levels, with experienced mentors to help everyone keep up.
targetAudience: Intermediate Python developers with basic LLM experience, welcoming to beginners

suggestedSpeakers.name: Sarah Chen
suggestedSpeakers.reason: RAG pipelines are her core expertise. Her blog-style teaching approach works well for mixed-level audiences.

suggestedSpeakers.name: Marcus Johnson
suggestedSpeakers.reason: Excels at making complex AI concepts accessible. Great at facilitating group exercises and keeping energy high.

suggestedSpeakers.name: Elena Vasquez
suggestedSpeakers.reason: Can bridge the gap between RAG as a technical concept and RAG as a user-facing product. Good for the 'why it matters' angle.

suggestedVenue.name: Community Maker Space
suggestedVenue.reason: Every seat has a monitor and power outlets. Capacity 40 matches target. Free parking makes it accessible for the community focus.

slots.title: What Is RAG and Why Should You Care?
slots.speaker: marcus-johnson

slots.title: Group Exercise: Build a Pipeline Together
slots.speaker: sarah-chen

slots.title: RAG in the Real World: User Perspectives
slots.speaker: elena-vasquez
